title: make associations to the random words I'm about to say one a

messages.text: make associations to the random words I'm about to say one at a time
ROSE Sure, go ahead and say your first word.
ROSE Bubble can be associated with various things like bath time, soap, popping sounds, or even champagne. What's your next word?
also connect one of the things you said to the next word I give you--that was not the last word I said. tell me the words you use: peter

ROSE When you mentioned "bubble," I associated it with bath time and soap. Now, for your word "Peter," a natural connection could be a character from stories or a name given to someone named after Saint Peter in Christian tradition.

What's your next word?

ROSE So, you're connecting the bubble to something that is gone, perhaps like bubbles dissolving or fading away. Now for your new word "gone," this could remind us of a sense of loss or the disappearance of something or someone.

What's your next word?
whats the first word I gave you?
ROSE The first word you gave me was "bubble."

ROSE So, if we're connecting "gone" with "bubble," and now using "shadow," it might evoke a feeling of something that is no longer present or has faded away, much like a shadow that disappears in sunlight.

What's your next word?
repeat the list of words I gave
ROSE You gave me the following words: gone, shadow.
